member.name: T:Datadog.iOS.DatadogURLSessionDelegate
member.summary: The URLSession delegate object which enables network requests instrumentation. It must be used together withDatadogRUM or DatadogTrace. All requests made with the URLSession instrumented with this delegate will be intercepted by the SDK.

member.name: C:Datadog.iOS.DatadogURLSessionDelegate(Foundation.NSSet{Foundation.NSString})
member.param: These hosts are tracked in addition to what was passed to DatadogConfiguration.Builder via trackURLSession(firstPartyHosts:)
member.summary: Automatically tracked hosts can be customized per instance with this initializer. NOTE: If trackURLSession(firstPartyHosts:) is never called, automatic tracking will not take place.

member.name: T:Datadog.iOS.DDB3HTTPHeadersWriter
member.summary: The DDB3HTTPHeadersWriter class facilitates the injection of trace propagation headers into network requests targeted at a backend expecting B3 propagation format.

remarks.para: See B3 propagation format (https://github.com/openzipkin/b3-propagation) for more details.
Usage:

member.name: P:Datadog.iOS.DDB3HTTPHeadersWriter.TraceHeaderFields
member.summary: A dictionary containing the required HTTP Headers for propagating trace information.

remarks.para: Usage:

member.name: T:Datadog.iOS.DDBatchProcessingLevel
member.summary: Defines the maximum amount of batches processed sequentially without a delay within one reading/uploading cycle.

member.name: T:Datadog.iOS.DDBatchSize
member.summary: Defines the Datadog SDK policy when batching data together before uploading it to Datadog servers.
member.remarks: Smaller batches mean smaller but more network requests, whereas larger batches mean fewer but larger network requests.

member.name: F:Datadog.iOS.DDBatchSize.Large
member.summary: Prefer large-sized data batches.

member.name: F:Datadog.iOS.DDBatchSize.Medium
member.summary: Prefer medium-sized data batches.

member.name: F:Datadog.iOS.DDBatchSize.Small
member.summary: Prefer small-sized data batches.

member.name: T:Datadog.iOS.DDConfiguration
member.summary: Configuration of the Datadog SDK.

member.name: C:Datadog.iOS.DDConfiguration(System.String,System.String)
member.param: Either the RUM client token (which supports RUM, Logging and APM) or regular client token, only for Logging and APM.
The environment name which will be sent to Datadog. This can be used to filter events on different environments (e.g. “staging” or “production”).
member.summary: Creates a Datadog SDK Configuration object.

member.name: P:Datadog.iOS.DDConfiguration.BatchProcessingLevel
member.summary: Batch processing level, defining the maximum number of batches processed sequentially without a delay within one reading/uploading cycle.
member.remarks: by default.

member.name: P:Datadog.iOS.DDConfiguration.BatchSize
member.summary: The preferred size of batched data uploaded to Datadog servers. This value impacts the size and number of requests performed by the SDK. Medium by default.

member.name: P:Datadog.iOS.DDConfiguration.Bundle
member.summary: The bundle object that contains the current executable.

member.name: P:Datadog.iOS.DDConfiguration.ClientToken
member.summary: Either the RUM client token (which supports RUM, Logging and APM) or regular client token, only for Logging and APM.

member.name: P:Datadog.iOS.DDConfiguration.Env
member.summary: The environment name which will be sent to Datadog. This can be used to filter events on different environments (e.g. “staging” or “production”).

member.name: P:Datadog.iOS.DDConfiguration.ProxyConfiguration
member.summary: Proxy configuration attributes. This can be used to a enable a custom proxy for uploading tracked data to Datadog's intake.

member.name: P:Datadog.iOS.DDConfiguration.Service
member.summary: The service name associated with data send to Datadog. Default value is set to application bundle identifier.

member.name: M:Datadog.iOS.DDConfiguration.SetEncryption(Datadog.iOS.IDDDataEncryption)
member.summary: Sets Data encryption to use for on-disk data persistency by providing an object complying with DataEncryption protocol.

member.name: M:Datadog.iOS.DDConfiguration.SetServerDateProvider(Datadog.iOS.IDDServerDateProvider)
member.summary: A custom NTP synchronization interface. By default, the Datadog SDK synchronizes with dedicated NTP pools provided by the https://www.ntppool.org/ . Using different pools or setting a no-op ServerDateProvider implementation will result in desynchronization of the SDK instance and the Datadog servers. This can lead to significant time shift in RUM sessions or distributed traces.

member.name: P:Datadog.iOS.DDConfiguration.Site
member.summary: The Datadog server site where data is sent. Default value is US1.

member.name: P:Datadog.iOS.DDConfiguration.UploadFrequency
member.summary: The preferred frequency of uploading data to Datadog servers. This value impacts the frequency of performing network requests by the SDK. Average by default.

member.name: T:Datadog.iOS.DDCrashReporter
member.summary: Enable iOS Crash Reporting and Error Tracking to get comprehensive crash reports and error trends with Real User Monitoring.

remarks.para: With this feature, you can access:

item.description: Aggregated iOS crash dashboards and attributes
Symbolicated iOS crash reports
Trend analysis with iOS error tracking

remarks.para: In order to symbolicate your stack traces, find and upload your .dSYM files to Datadog. Then, verify your configuration by running a test crash and restarting your application. Your crash reports appear in Error Tracking (https://app.datadoghq.com/rum/error-tracking).

member.name: M:Datadog.iOS.DDCrashReporter.Enable
member.summary: Initializes the Datadog Crash Reporter.

member.name: T:Datadog.iOS.DDDatadog
member.summary: An entry point to the Datadog SDK.

remarks.para: Initialize the core instance of the Datadog SDK prior to enabling any product.
Once the Datadog SDK is initialized, you can enable products such as RUM:

member.name: M:Datadog.iOS.DDDatadog.AddUserExtraInfo(Foundation.NSDictionary{Foundation.NSString,Foundation.NSObject})
member.param: User's additional custom attributes.
member.summary: Add custom attributes to the current user information.

remarks.para: This extra info will be added to already existing extra info that is added to logs traces and RUM events automatically.

member.name: M:Datadog.iOS.DDDatadog.ClearAllData
member.summary: xClears all data that has not already been sent to the Datadog servers.

member.name: M:Datadog.iOS.DDDatadog.Initialize(Datadog.iOS.DDConfiguration,Datadog.iOS.DDTrackingConsent)
member.param: The SDK configuration of type .
The initial state of the Data Tracking Consent () given by the user of the app.
member.summary: Initializes the Datadog SDK.

remarks.para: You must initialize the core instance of the Datadog SDK prior to enabling any product.
Once the Datadog SDK is initialized, you can enable products such as RUM:

member.name: M:Datadog.iOS.DDDatadog.SetTrackingConsent(Datadog.iOS.DDTrackingConsent)
member.param: The new consent value, which will be applied for all data collected from now on.
member.summary: Sets the tracking consent regarding the data collection for the Datadog SDK.

member.name: M:Datadog.iOS.DDDatadog.SetUserInfo(System.String,System.String,System.String,Foundation.NSDictionary{Foundation.NSString,Foundation.NSObject})
member.param: User ID, if any.
Name representing the user, if any.
User's e-mail address, if any.
User's custom attributes, if any.
member.summary: Sets current user information.

remarks.para: Those will be added to logs, traces and RUM events automatically.

member.name: M:Datadog.iOS.DDDatadog.StopInstance
member.summary: Stops the initialized SDK instance.

remarks.para: Stopping a core instance will stop all current processes by deallocating all Features registered in the core as well as their storage & upload units.

member.name: P:Datadog.iOS.DDDatadog.VerbosityLevel
member.summary: Verbosity level of the Datadog SDK. Can be used for debugging purposes.

remarks.para: If set, internal events occuring inside the SDK will be printed to the debugger console if their level is equal or greater than VerbosityLevel.
Default is null.

member.name: T:Datadog.iOS.DDDefaultUIKitRUMViewsPredicate
member.summary: Default implementation of . It names RUM Views by the names of their subclasses.

member.name: T:Datadog.iOS.DDHTTPHeadersWriter
member.summary: The HTTPHeadersWriter class facilitates the injection of trace propagation headers into network requests targeted at a backend instrumented with Datadog and expecting x-datadog-* headers.

remarks.para: Usage:

member.name: P:Datadog.iOS.DDHTTPHeadersWriter.TraceHeaderFields
member.summary: A dictionary containing the required HTTP Headers for propagating trace information.

remarks.para: Usage:

member.name: T:Datadog.iOS.DDLogger
member.summary: Datadog Logger.

remarks.para: Usage:

member.name: M:Datadog.iOS.DDLogger.Add(System.String)
member.param: Value of the tag.
member.summary: Adds the tag to all future logs sent by this logger.

remarks.para: Tags must start with a letter and:

item.description: may contain: alphanumerics, underscores, minuses, colons, periods, and slashes;
other special characters are converted to underscores;
must be lowercase;
and can be at most 200 characters long (tags exceeding this limit will be truncated to first 200 characters).

remarks.para: See also: Defining Tags (https://docs.datadoghq.com/tagging/#defining-tags)

member.name: M:Datadog.iOS.DDLogger.AddAttribute(System.String,Foundation.NSObject)
member.summary: Adds a custom attribute to all future logs sent by this logger.

member.name: M:Datadog.iOS.DDLogger.AddTag(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Adds a "key:value" tag to all future logs sent by this logger.

remarks.para: Tags must start with a letter and:

item.description: may contain: alphanumerics, underscores, minuses, colons, periods, and slashes;
other special characters are converted to underscores;
must be lowercase;
and can be at most 200 characters long (tags exceeding this limit will be truncated to first 200 characters).

remarks.para: See also: Defining Tags (https://docs.datadoghq.com/tagging/#defining-tags)

member.name: M:Datadog.iOS.DDLogger.Critical(System.String)
member.param: The message to be logged.
member.summary: Sends a CRITICAL log message.

member.name: M:Datadog.iOS.DDLogger.Critical(System.String,Foundation.NSDictionary{Foundation.NSString,Foundation.NSObject})
member.param: The message to be logged.
A dictionary of attributes to add for this message. If an attribute with the same key already exists in this logger, it will be overridden (only for this message).
member.summary: Sends a CRITICAL log message.

member.name: M:Datadog.iOS.DDLogger.Critical(System.String,Foundation.NSError,Foundation.NSDictionary{Foundation.NSString,Foundation.NSObject})
member.param: The message to be logged.
The error information.
A dictionary of attributes to add for this message. If an attribute with the same key already exists in this logger, it will be overridden (only for this message).
member.summary: Sends a CRITICAL log message.

member.name: M:Datadog.iOS.DDLogger.Debug(System.String)
member.param: The message to be logged.
member.summary: Sends a DEBUG log message.

member.name: M:Datadog.iOS.DDLogger.Debug(System.String,Foundation.NSDictionary{Foundation.NSString,Foundation.NSObject})
member.param: The message to be logged.
A dictionary of attributes to add for this message. If an attribute with the same key already exist in this logger, it will be overridden (only for this message).
member.summary: Sends a DEBUG log message.

member.name: M:Datadog.iOS.DDLogger.Debug(System.String,Foundation.NSError,Foundation.NSDictionary{Foundation.NSString,Foundation.NSObject})
member.param: The message to be logged.
The error information.
A dictionary of attributes to add for this message. If an attribute with the same key already exist in this logger, it will be overridden (only for this message).
member.summary: Sends a DEBUG log message.

member.name: M:Datadog.iOS.DDLogger.Error(System.String)
member.param: The message to be logged.
member.summary: Sends an ERROR log message.

member.name: M:Datadog.iOS.DDLogger.Error(System.String,Foundation.NSDictionary{Foundation.NSString,Foundation.NSObject})
member.param: The message to be logged.
A dictionary of attributes to add for this message. If an attribute with the same key already exist in this logger, it will be overridden (only for this message).
member.summary: Sends an ERROR log message.

member.name: M:Datadog.iOS.DDLogger.Error(System.String,Foundation.NSError,Foundation.NSDictionary{Foundation.NSString,Foundation.NSObject})
member.param: The message to be logged.
The error information.
A dictionary of attributes to add for this message. If an attribute with the same key already exist in this logger, it will be overridden (only for this message).
member.summary: Sends a ERROR log message.

member.name: M:Datadog.iOS.DDLogger.Remove(System.String)
member.param: The value of the tag to remove.
member.summary: Removes the tag from all future logs sent by this logger. Previous logs won't lose the this tag if created prior to this call.

member.name: M:Datadog.iOS.DDLogger.RemoveAttribute(System.String)
member.summary: Removes the custom attribute from all future logs sent by this logger. Previous logs won't lose this attribute if sent prior to this call.

member.name: M:Datadog.iOS.DDLogger.RemoveTag(System.String)
member.param: The key of the tag to remove.
member.summary: Remove all tags with the given key from all future logs sent by this logger. Previous logs won't lose this tag if created prior to this call.

member.name: T:Datadog.iOS.DDLoggerConfiguration
member.summary: Datadog logger.

member.name: C:Datadog.iOS.DDLoggerConfiguration(System.String,System.String,System.Boolean,System.Boolean,System.Boolean,System.Single,Datadog.iOS.DDLogLevel,System.Boolean)
member.param: The service name (default value is set to the application bundle identifier).
The logger custom name (default value is set to the main bundle identifier).
Enriches logs with network connection info. false by default.
Enables the logs integration with RUM. true by default.
Enables the logs integration with the active span API from Tracing. true by default.
The sample rate for remote logging. When set to 0, no log entries will be sent to Datadog servers.
Set the minimum log level reported to Datadog servers. Debug by default.
Format to use when printing logs to the console - either Short or Json.
member.summary: Creates a Logger Configuration.

member.name: P:Datadog.iOS.DDLoggerConfiguration.BundleWithRumEnabled
member.summary: Enables the logs integration with RUM. If enabled all the logs will be enriched with the current RUM View information and it will be possible to see all the logs sent during a specific View lifespan in the RUM Explorer. true by default.

member.name: P:Datadog.iOS.DDLoggerConfiguration.BundleWithTraceEnabled
member.summary: Enables the logs integration with active span API from Tracing. If enabled all the logs will be bundled with the DDTracer.Shared.activeSpan trace and it will be possible to see all the logs sent during that specific trace. true by default.

member.name: P:Datadog.iOS.DDLoggerConfiguration.Name
member.summary: The logger custom name (default value is set to the main bundle identifier).

member.name: P:Datadog.iOS.DDLoggerConfiguration.NetworkInfoEnabled
member.summary: Enriches logs with network connection info. This means: reachability status, connection type, mobile carrier name and many more will be added to each log. For full list of network info attributes see NetworkConnectionInfo and CarrierInfo. false by default.

member.name: P:Datadog.iOS.DDLoggerConfiguration.PrintLogsToConsole
member.summary: Enables logs to be printed to the debugger console. false by default.

member.name: P:Datadog.iOS.DDLoggerConfiguration.RemoteLogThreshold
member.summary: Set the minimum log level reported to Datadog servers. Any log with a level equal or above the threshold will be sent. Note: this setting doesn't impact logs printed to the console if is used - all logs will be printed, no matter their level. DDLogLevel.Debug by default.

member.name: P:Datadog.iOS.DDLoggerConfiguration.RemoteSampleRate
member.summary: Sets the sampling rate for logging. The sampling rate must be a value between 0 and 100. A value of 0 means no logs will be processed, 100 means all logs will be processed. By default sampling is disabled, meaning that all logs are being processed.

member.name: P:Datadog.iOS.DDLoggerConfiguration.Service
member.summary: The service name (default value is set to the application bundle identifier).

member.name: T:Datadog.iOS.DDLogLevel
member.summary: Log levels ordered by their severity, with Debug being the least severe and Critical being the most severe.

member.name: T:Datadog.iOS.DDLogs
member.summary: iOS Log Collection

remarks.para: Send logs to Datadog from your iOS applications with Datadog's client-side logging library and leverage the following features:

item.term: Log to Datadog in JSON format natively.
Use default and add custom attributes to each log sent.
Record real client IP addresses and User-Agents.
Leverage optimized network usage with automatic bulk posts.

member.name: M:Datadog.iOS.DDLogs.AddAttribute(System.String,Foundation.NSObject)
member.summary: Adds a custom attribute to all future logs sent by any logger created from the provided Core.

member.name: M:Datadog.iOS.DDLogs.RemoveAttribute(System.String)
member.param: The key of an attribute that will be removed.
member.summary: Removes the custom attribute from all future logs sent. Previous logs won't lose this attribute if sent prior to this call.

member.name: T:Datadog.iOS.DDLogsConfiguration
member.summary: The Logs general configuration.
member.remarks: This configuration will be applied to all DDLogs instances.

member.name: T:Datadog.iOS.DDNSURLSessionDelegate
member.summary: Configuration of URLSession instrumentation.

member.name: C:Datadog.iOS.DDNSURLSessionDelegate(Foundation.NSDictionary{Foundation.NSString,Foundation.NSSet{Datadog.iOS.DDTracingHeaderType}})
member.param: Dictionary of hosts and tracing header types to use. Note: Hosts must not include "http(s)://" prefix.

member.name: T:Datadog.iOS.DDRUM
member.summary: An entry point to the Datadog RUM feature.

member.name: M:Datadog.iOS.DDRUM.Enable(Datadog.iOS.DDRUMConfiguration)
member.param: Configuration of the feature.
member.summary: Enables the Datadog RUM feature.
member.remarks: After RUM is enabled, use to collect RUM events.

member.name: C:Datadog.iOS.DDRUMAction(System.String,Foundation.NSDictionary{Foundation.NSString,Foundation.NSObject})
member.param: The RUM Action name, appearing as ACTION NAME in RUM Explorer.
Additional attributes to associate with the RUM Action.
member.summary: Initializes the RUM Action description.

member.name: T:Datadog.iOS.DDRUMConfiguration
member.summary: RUM feature configuration.

member.name: P:Datadog.iOS.DDRUMConfiguration.AppHangThreshold
member.summary: Enables App Hangs monitoring with the given threshold (in seconds).

remarks.para: Only App Hangs that last more than this threshold will be reported. The minimal allowed value for this option is 0.1 seconds. To disable hangs monitoring, set this parameter to nil.

Note: Be cautious when setting the threshold to very small values, as it may lead to excessive reporting of hangs. The SDK implements a secondary thread for monitoring App Hangs. To reduce CPU utilization, it tracks hangs with a tolerance of 2.5%, meaning that some hangs lasting very close to this threshold may not be reported.
Note: App Hangs monitoring requires Datadog Crash Reporting to be enabled. Otherwise stack trace will be not reported in App Hang errors.
Default: NULL (hangs monitoring disabled).

member.name: P:Datadog.iOS.DDRUMConfiguration.ApplicationId
member.summary: An unique identifier of the RUM application in Datadog.

member.name: P:Datadog.iOS.DDRUMConfiguration.CustomEndpoint
member.summary: Custom server URL for sending RUM data.

remarks.para: Default: null.

member.name: P:Datadog.iOS.DDRUMConfiguration.LongTaskThreshold
member.summary: Enables RUM long tasks tracking with the given threshold (in seconds).

remarks.para: Any operation on the main thread that exceeds this threshold will be reported as a RUM long task. To disable RUM long tasks tracking, set null or 0.
Default: 0.1.

member.name: P:Datadog.iOS.DDRUMConfiguration.OnSessionStart
member.summary: RUM session start callback.

remarks.para: It takes 2 arguments: - Newly started session ID. - Flag indicating whether or not the session was discarded due to the sampling rate.
Keep the implementation fast and do not make any assumptions on the thread that runs this callback.
Default: null.

member.name: P:Datadog.iOS.DDRUMConfiguration.SessionSampleRate
member.summary: The sampling rate for RUM sessions.

remarks.para: It must be a number between 0.0 and 100.0, where 0 means no sessions will be sent and 100 means all will be uploaded.
Default: 100.0.

member.name: M:Datadog.iOS.DDRUMConfiguration.SetActionEventMapper(System.Func{Datadog.iOS.DDRUMActionEvent,Datadog.iOS.DDRUMActionEvent})
member.summary: Sets a custom mapper for RUM action events.

remarks.para: It can be used to modify action events before they are sent. The implementation of the mapper should obtain a mutable copy of , modify it, and return it. Returning null will drop the event. Keep the implementation fast and do not make any assumptions on the thread used to run it.
Default: null.

member.name: M:Datadog.iOS.DDRUMConfiguration.SetErrorEventMapper(System.Func{Datadog.iOS.DDRUMErrorEvent,Datadog.iOS.DDRUMErrorEvent})
member.summary: Sets a custom mapper for RUM error events.

remarks.para: It can be used to modify error events before they are sent. The implementation of the mapper should obtain a mutable copy of , modify it, and return it. Returning null will drop the event. Keep the implementation fast and do not make any assumptions on the thread used to run it.
Default: null.

member.name: M:Datadog.iOS.DDRUMConfiguration.SetLongTaskEventMapper(System.Func{Datadog.iOS.DDRUMLongTaskEvent,Datadog.iOS.DDRUMLongTaskEvent})
member.summary: Sets a custom mapper for RUM long task events.

remarks.para: It can be used to modify long task events before they are sent. The implementation of the mapper should obtain a mutable copy of , modify it, and return it. Returning null will drop the event. Keep the implementation fast and do not make any assumptions on the thread used to run it.
Default: null.

member.name: M:Datadog.iOS.DDRUMConfiguration.SetResourceEventMapper(System.Func{Datadog.iOS.DDRUMResourceEvent,Datadog.iOS.DDRUMResourceEvent})
member.summary: Custom mapper for RUM resource events.

remarks.para: It can be used to modify resource events before they are sent. The implementation of the mapper should obtain a mutable copy of , modify it, and return it. Returning null will drop the event. Keep the implementation fast and do not make any assumptions on the thread used to run it.
Default: null.

member.name: M:Datadog.iOS.DDRUMConfiguration.SetURLSessionTracking(Datadog.iOS.DDRUMURLSessionTracking)
member.summary: The configuration for automatic RUM resources tracking.

remarks.para: RUM resources tracking requires enabling . See .
Default: null - which means automatic RUM resource tracking is not enabled by default.

member.name: M:Datadog.iOS.DDRUMConfiguration.SetViewEventMapper(System.Func{Datadog.iOS.DDRUMViewEvent,Datadog.iOS.DDRUMViewEvent})
member.summary: Sets a custom mapper for RUM view events.

remarks.para: It can be used to modify view events before they are sent. The implementation of the mapper should obtain a mutable copy of , modify it, and return it. Keep the implementation fast and do not make any assumptions on the thread used to run it.

Note: This mapper ensures that all views are sent by preventing the return of null. To drop certain automatically collected RUM views, adjust the implementation of the view predicate (see the option).
Default: null.

member.name: P:Datadog.iOS.DDRUMConfiguration.TelemetrySampleRate
member.summary: The sampling rate for SDK internal telemetry utilized by Datadog.

remarks.para: This telemetry is used to monitor the internal workings of the entire Datadog iOS SDK.
It must be a number between 0.0 and 100.0, where 0 means no telemetry will be sent, and 100 means all will be uploaded.
The default value is 20.0.

member.name: P:Datadog.iOS.DDRUMConfiguration.TrackBackgroundEvents
member.summary: Determines whether RUM events should be tracked when no view is active (including when the app is in the background).

remarks.para: If enabled, RUM will attach events to an automatically created "background" view.
Note: Enabling this option may increase the number of sessions tracked and result in higher billing.
Default: false.

member.name: P:Datadog.iOS.DDRUMConfiguration.TrackFrustrations
member.summary: Determines whether automatic tracking of user frustrations should be enabled.

remarks.para: RUM detects "error taps" when an error follows a RUM tap action.
Default: true.

member.name: P:Datadog.iOS.DDRUMConfiguration.TrackWatchdogTerminations
member.summary: Determines whether the SDK should track application termination by the watchdog.

remarks.para: Read more about watchdog terminations at https://developer.apple.com/documentation/xcode/addressing-watchdog-terminations
Default: false.

member.name: P:Datadog.iOS.DDRUMConfiguration.UIKitActionsPredicate
member.summary: The predicate for automatically tracking events as RUM actions.

remarks.para: RUM will query this predicate for each that the user interacts with. The predicate implementation should return RUM action parameters if the given interaction should be accepted, or null to ignore it. Touch events on the keyboard are ignored for privacy reasons.
You can use or create your own predicate by implementing .
Note: Automatic RUM action tracking involves swizzling the UIApplication.SendEvent() method.
Default: null - which means automatic RUM action tracking is not enabled by default.

member.name: P:Datadog.iOS.DDRUMConfiguration.UIKitViewsPredicate
member.summary: The predicate for automatically tracking UIViewControllers as RUM views.

remarks.para: RUM will query this predicate for each UIViewController presented in the app. The predicate implementation should return RUM view parameters if the given controller should start a view, or null to ignore it.
You can use or create your own predicate by implementing .
Note: Automatic RUM views tracking involves swizzling the UIViewController lifecycle methods.
Default: null - which means automatic RUM view tracking is not enabled by default.

member.name: P:Datadog.iOS.DDRUMConfiguration.VitalsUpdateFrequency
member.summary: Sets the preferred frequency for collecting RUM vitals.

remarks.para: To disable RUM vitals monitoring, set null.
Default: Average.

member.name: F:Datadog.iOS.DDRUMErrorSource.Console
member.summary: Error originated in a web console (used by bridges).

member.name: F:Datadog.iOS.DDRUMErrorSource.Custom
member.summary: Custom error source.

member.name: F:Datadog.iOS.DDRUMErrorSource.Network
member.summary: Error originated in the network layer.

member.name: F:Datadog.iOS.DDRUMErrorSource.Source
member.summary: Error originated in the source code.

member.name: F:Datadog.iOS.DDRUMErrorSource.Webview
member.summary: Error originated in a webview.

member.name: T:Datadog.iOS.DDRUMFirstPartyHostsTracing
member.summary: Defines configuration for first-party hosts in distributed tracing.

member.name: T:Datadog.iOS.DDRUMMonitor
member.summary: A class for manual interaction with the RUM feature. It records RUM events that are sent to Datadog RUM.

remarks.para: There can be only one active RUM monitor for a particular instance of the Datadog SDK. It gets enabled along with the call to DDRUM.Enable (DDRUMConfiguration configuration):

member.name: M:Datadog.iOS.DDRUMMonitor.AddAction(Datadog.iOS.DDRUMActionType,System.String,Foundation.NSDictionary{Foundation.NSString,Foundation.NSObject})
member.param: The type of the action.
The name of the action.
Custom attributes to attach to this action.
member.summary: Adds a RUM action that has no duration.

member.name: M:Datadog.iOS.DDRUMMonitor.AddAttribute(System.String,Foundation.NSObject)
member.param: The key for this attribute. See documentation for information about nesting attribute values using dot . syntax.
Any value. See documentation for information about nested encoding containers limitation.
member.summary: Adds a custom attribute to next RUM events.

member.name: M:Datadog.iOS.DDRUMMonitor.AddError(Foundation.NSError,Datadog.iOS.DDRUMErrorSource,Foundation.NSDictionary{Foundation.NSString,Foundation.NSObject})
member.param: The object. It will be used to infer error details.
The origin of the error.
Custom attributes to attach to this error.
member.summary: Adds a RUM error to the current RUM view.

member.name: M:Datadog.iOS.DDRUMMonitor.AddError(System.String,System.String,Datadog.iOS.DDRUMErrorSource,Foundation.NSDictionary{Foundation.NSString,Foundation.NSObject})
member.param: The error message.
The stack trace of the error. No specific format is required. If not specified, it will be inferred from `file` and `line`.
The origin of the error.
Custom attributes to attach to this error.
member.summary: Adds a RUM error to the current RUM view.

member.name: M:Datadog.iOS.DDRUMMonitor.AddFeatureFlagEvaluationWithName(System.String,Foundation.NSObject)
member.param: The name of the feature flag.
The result of the evaluation.
member.summary: Adds the result of evaluating a feature flag to the current RUM view.
member.remarks: Feature flag evaluations are local to the active view and are cleared when the view is stopped.

member.name: M:Datadog.iOS.DDRUMMonitor.AddResourceMetrics(System.String,Foundation.NSUrlSessionTaskMetrics,Foundation.NSDictionary{Foundation.NSString,Foundation.NSObject})
member.param: The key representing the resource. It must match the one used to start the resource.
The NSUrlSessionTaskMetrics for this resource.
Custom attributes to attach to this resource.
member.summary: Adds temporal metrics to given RUM resource.

member.name: M:Datadog.iOS.DDRUMMonitor.AddTiming(System.String)
member.param: The name of the custom timing attribute. It must be unique for each timing.
member.summary: Records a specific timing within the current RUM view.
member.remarks: The duration of the timing is calculated as the number of nanoseconds elapsed between the start of the view and the addition of the timing.

member.name: P:Datadog.iOS.DDRUMMonitor.Debug
member.summary: Enable a debug utility to inspect the active RUM view. Use it only when debugging.
member.remarks: If enabled, a debugging outline will be displayed on top of the application, indicating the name of the active RUM view. This can be helpful for debugging RUM instrumentation issues in your app.

member.name: M:Datadog.iOS.DDRUMMonitor.GetCurrentSessionIdViaCallback(System.Action{Foundation.NSString})
member.param: The callback that will recieve the current session ID. This will be called from a background thread.
member.summary: Get the currently active session ID. Returns null if no sessions are currently active or if the current session is sampled out.

remarks.para: This method uses an asynchronous callback to ensure all pending RUM events have been processed up to the moment of the call.

member.name: M:Datadog.iOS.DDRUMMonitor.RemoveAttribute(System.String)
member.param: The key for the attribute that will be removed.
member.summary: Removes an attribute from next RUM events.
member.remarks: Events created prior to this call will not lose this attribute.

member.name: P:Datadog.iOS.DDRUMMonitor.Shared
member.summary: Obtains the RUM monitor for manual interaction with the RUM feature.
member.remarks: It requires to be called first - otherwise it will return a no-op implementation.

member.name: M:Datadog.iOS.DDRUMMonitor.StartAction(Datadog.iOS.DDRUMActionType,System.String,Foundation.NSDictionary{Foundation.NSString,Foundation.NSObject})
member.param: The type of the action.
The name of the action.
Custom attributes to attach to this action.
member.summary: Starts a RUM action.
member.remarks: If the action is not stopped with , it will be stopped automatically after 10 seconds.

member.name: M:Datadog.iOS.DDRUMMonitor.StartResource(System.String,Foundation.NSUrl,Foundation.NSDictionary{Foundation.NSString,Foundation.NSObject})
member.param: The key representing the resource. It must be unique among all resources being currently tracked.
The NSUrl of this resource.
Custom attributes to attach to this resource.
member.summary: Starts a RUM resource.

member.name: M:Datadog.iOS.DDRUMMonitor.StartResource(System.String,Foundation.NSUrlRequest,Foundation.NSDictionary{Foundation.NSString,Foundation.NSObject})
member.param: The key representing the resource. It must be unique among all resources being currently tracked.
The NSUrlRequest of this resource.
Custom attributes to attach to this resource.
member.summary: Starts a RUM resource.

member.name: M:Datadog.iOS.DDRUMMonitor.StartResource(System.String,Datadog.iOS.DDRUMMethod,System.String,Foundation.NSDictionary{Foundation.NSString,Foundation.NSObject})
member.param: The key representing the resource. It must be unique among all resources being currently tracked.
A representing the HTTP method of this resource.
The URL string of this resource.
Custom attributes to attach to this resource.
member.summary: Starts a RUM resource.

member.name: M:Datadog.iOS.DDRUMMonitor.StartView(System.String,System.String,Foundation.NSDictionary{Foundation.NSString,Foundation.NSObject})
member.param: A string value identifying this view. It must match the key passed later to .
The name of the view. If not provided, the name will be used.
Custom attributes to attach to this view.
member.summary: Starts the RUM view.

member.name: M:Datadog.iOS.DDRUMMonitor.StartView(UIKit.UIViewController,System.String,Foundation.NSDictionary{Foundation.NSString,Foundation.NSObject})
member.param: The instance of UIViewController representing this view.
The name of the view. If not provided, the class name will be used.
Custom attributes to attach to this view.
member.summary: Starts the RUM view.

member.name: M:Datadog.iOS.DDRUMMonitor.StopAction(Datadog.iOS.DDRUMActionType,System.String,Foundation.NSDictionary{Foundation.NSString,Foundation.NSObject})
member.param: The type of the action. It should match the type passed when starting this action.
The name of the action. If not provided, it will use the name the action was started with.
Custom attributes to attach to this action.
member.summary: Stops a RUM action.
member.remarks: The action must be first started with .

member.name: M:Datadog.iOS.DDRUMMonitor.StopResource(System.String,Foundation.NSError,Foundation.NSUrlResponse,Foundation.NSDictionary{Foundation.NSString,Foundation.NSObject})
member.param: The key representing the resource. It must match the one used to start the resource.
The NSError object. It will be used to infer error details.
An optional NSUrlResponse received for the resource.
Custom attributes to attach to this resource.
member.summary: Stops a RUM resource and reports an error.

member.name: M:Datadog.iOS.DDRUMMonitor.StopResource(System.String,Foundation.NSUrlResponse,Foundation.NSNumber,Foundation.NSDictionary{Foundation.NSString,Foundation.NSObject})
member.param: The key representing the resource. It must match the one used to start the resource.
The NSUrlResponse received for the resource.
An optional size of the data received for the resource (in bytes). If not provided, it will be inferred from the "Content-Length" header of the response.
Custom attributes to attach to this resource.
member.summary: Stops a RUM resource.

member.name: M:Datadog.iOS.DDRUMMonitor.StopResource(System.String,System.String,Foundation.NSUrlResponse,Foundation.NSDictionary{Foundation.NSString,Foundation.NSObject})
member.param: The key representing the resource. It must match the one used to start the resource.
The message explaining the Resource failure.
An optional NSUrlResponse received for the resource.
Custom attributes to attach to this resource.
member.summary: Stops a RUM resource and reports an error.

member.name: M:Datadog.iOS.DDRUMMonitor.StopResource(System.String,Foundation.NSNumber,Datadog.iOS.DDRUMResourceType,Foundation.NSNumber,Foundation.NSDictionary{Foundation.NSString,Foundation.NSObject})
member.param: The key representing the resource. It must match the one used to start the resource.
HTTP code of the response.
Type of the resource.
An optional size of the data received for the resource (in bytes).
Custom attributes to attach to this resource.
member.summary: Stops a RUM resource.

member.name: M:Datadog.iOS.DDRUMMonitor.StopSession
member.summary: Stops the current RUM session.
member.remarks: A new session will start in response to a call to or . If the session is started because of a call to AddAction, the last known view is restarted in the new session.

member.name: M:Datadog.iOS.DDRUMMonitor.StopView(System.String,Foundation.NSDictionary{Foundation.NSString,Foundation.NSObject})
member.param: A string value identifying this view. It must match the key passed earlier to .
Custom attributes to attach to this view.
member.summary: Stops the RUM view.

member.name: M:Datadog.iOS.DDRUMMonitor.StopView(UIKit.UIViewController,Foundation.NSDictionary{Foundation.NSString,Foundation.NSObject})
member.param: The instance of UIViewController representing this view.
Custom attributes to attach to this view.
member.summary: Stops the RUM view.

member.name: T:Datadog.iOS.DDRUMURLSessionTracking
member.summary: Configuration of automatic RUM resources tracking.

member.name: M:Datadog.iOS.DDRUMURLSessionTracking.SetFirstPartyHostsTracing(Datadog.iOS.DDRUMFirstPartyHostsTracing)
member.summary: Determines distributed tracing configuration for particular first-party hosts.

remarks.para: Each request is classified as first-party or third-party based on the first-party hosts configured.
For example:

item.description: If "example.com" is defined as a first-party host:
First-party URL examples: https://example.com/ and https://api.example.com/v2/users
Third-party URL example: https://foo.com/
If "api.example.com" is defined as a first-party host:
First-party URL examples: https://api.example.com/ and https://api.example.com/v2/users
Third-party URL examples: https://example.com/ and https://foo.com/

remarks.para: RUM will create a trace for each first-party resource by injecting HTTP trace headers and creating an APM span. If your backend is also instrumented with Datadog, you will see the full trace (app → backend).
Default: NULL - which means distributed tracing is not enabled by default.

member.name: M:Datadog.iOS.DDRUMURLSessionTracking.SetResourceAttributesProvider(System.Func{Foundation.NSUrlRequest,Foundation.NSUrlResponse,Foundation.NSData,Foundation.NSError,Foundation.NSDictionary{Foundation.NSString,Foundation.NSObject}})
member.param: Delegate for each network request.
member.summary: Custom attributes provider for intercepted RUM resources.

remarks.para: This delegate gets called for each network request intercepted by RUM. Use it to return additional attributes for RUM resource based on the provided request, response, data, and error. Keep the implementation fast and do not make any assumptions on the thread used to run it.
Note: This is not supported for (Swift) async-await APIs.
Default: NULL.

member.name: T:Datadog.iOS.DDRUMView
member.summary: A description of the RUM View returned from the DDUIKitRUMViewsPredicate.

member.name: C:Datadog.iOS.DDRUMView(System.String,Foundation.NSDictionary{Foundation.NSString,Foundation.NSObject})
member.param: The RUM View name, appearing as VIEW NAME in RUM Explorer.
Additional attributes to associate with the RUM View.
member.summary: Initializes the RUM View description.

member.name: P:Datadog.iOS.DDRUMView.Attributes
member.summary: Additional attributes to associate with the RUM View.

member.name: P:Datadog.iOS.DDRUMView.Name
member.summary: The RUM View name, appearing as VIEW NAME in RUM Explorer.

member.name: T:Datadog.iOS.DDRUMVitalsFrequency
member.summary: Frequency for collecting RUM vitals.

member.name: F:Datadog.iOS.DDRUMVitalsFrequency.Average
member.summary: Every 500ms.

member.name: F:Datadog.iOS.DDRUMVitalsFrequency.Never
member.summary: Never.

member.name: F:Datadog.iOS.DDRUMVitalsFrequency.Rare
member.summary: Every 1000ms.

member.name: T:Datadog.iOS.DDSessionReplay
member.summary: An entry point to the Datadog Session Replay feature.

member.name: M:Datadog.iOS.DDSessionReplay.Enable(Datadog.iOS.DDSessionReplayConfiguration)
member.param: TConfiguration of the feature.
member.summary: Enables Datadog Session Replay feature.

remarks.para: Recording will start automatically after enabling Session Replay.
Note: Session Replay requires the RUM feature to be enabled.

member.name: T:Datadog.iOS.DDSessionReplayConfiguration
member.summary: Session Replay feature configuration.

member.name: C:Datadog.iOS.DDSessionReplayConfiguration(System.Single)
member.param: The sampling rate for Session Replay. It is applied in addition to the RUM session sample rate.
member.summary: Creates Session Replay configuration.

member.name: P:Datadog.iOS.DDSessionReplayConfiguration.CustomEndpoint
member.summary: Custom server url for sending replay data. Default: NULL.

member.name: P:Datadog.iOS.DDSessionReplayConfiguration.DefaultPrivacyLevel
member.summary: Defines the way sensitive content (e.g. text) should be masked. Default: .mask.

member.name: P:Datadog.iOS.DDSessionReplayConfiguration.ReplaySampleRate
member.summary: The sampling rate for Session Replay. It is applied in addition to the RUM session sample rate. It must be a number between 0.0 and 100.0, where 0 means no replays will be recorded and 100 means all RUM sessions will contain replay.
member.remarks: Note: This sample rate is applied in addition to the RUM sample rate. For example, if RUM uses a sample rate of 80% and Session Replay uses a sample rate of 20%, it means that out of all user sessions, 80% will be included in RUM, and within those sessions, only 20% will have replays.

member.name: T:Datadog.iOS.DDSessionReplayConfigurationPrivacyLevel
member.summary: Available privacy levels for content masking.

member.name: F:Datadog.iOS.DDSessionReplayConfigurationPrivacyLevel.Allow
member.summary: Record all content.

member.name: F:Datadog.iOS.DDSessionReplayConfigurationPrivacyLevel.Mask
member.summary: Mask all content.

member.name: F:Datadog.iOS.DDSessionReplayConfigurationPrivacyLevel.MaskUserInput
member.summary: Mask input elements, but record all other content.

member.name: P:Datadog.iOS.DDSite.AP1
member.summary: Asia-based servers.
member.remarks: Sends data to https://ap1.datadoghq.com/

member.name: P:Datadog.iOS.DDSite.EU1
member.summary: Europe-based servers.
member.remarks: Sends data to https://app.datadoghq.eu/

member.name: P:Datadog.iOS.DDSite.US1
member.summary: US-based servers.
member.remarks: Sends data to https://app.datadoghq.com/

member.name: P:Datadog.iOS.DDSite.US1_Fed
member.summary: US-based servers, FedRAMP compatible.
member.remarks: Sends data to https://app.ddog-gov.com/

member.name: P:Datadog.iOS.DDSite.US3
member.summary: US-based servers.
member.remarks: Sends data to https://us3.datadoghq.com/

member.name: P:Datadog.iOS.DDSite.US5
member.summary: US-based servers.
member.remarks: Sends data to https://us5.datadoghq.com/

member.name: M:Datadog.iOS.DDTrace.Enable(Datadog.iOS.DDTraceConfiguration)
member.param: Configuration of the feature.
member.summary: Enables Datadog Trace feature.
member.remarks: After Trace is enabled, use DDTracer.Shared() to collect spans.

member.name: T:Datadog.iOS.DDTraceConfiguration
member.summary: Trace feature configuration.

member.name: P:Datadog.iOS.DDTraceConfiguration.BundleWithRumEnabled
member.summary: Enables the integration of traces with RUM. When enabled, all spans will be enriched with the current RUM view information.
member.remarks: Default: true.

member.name: P:Datadog.iOS.DDTraceConfiguration.ClassHandle
member.summary: Custom server url for sending traces.
member.remarks: Default: nil.

member.name: P:Datadog.iOS.DDTraceConfiguration.CustomEndpoint
member.summary: Custom server url for sending traces.
member.remarks: Default: NULL.

member.name: P:Datadog.iOS.DDTraceConfiguration.NetworkInfoEnabled
member.summary: Enriches traces with network connection information.

remarks.para: This includes reachability status, connection type, mobile carrier name, and more, which will be added to every span and span log.
Default: false.

member.name: P:Datadog.iOS.DDTraceConfiguration.SampleRate
member.summary: The sampling rate for spans created with the default tracer. It must be a number between 0.0 and 100.0, where 0 means no spans will be collected.
member.remarks: Default: 100.0.

member.name: P:Datadog.iOS.DDTraceConfiguration.Service
member.summary: The service value for spans. If not specified, the SDK default service will be used.

member.name: M:Datadog.iOS.DDTraceConfiguration.SetURLSessionTracking(Datadog.iOS.DDTraceURLSessionTracking)

summary.para: The configuration for automatic network requests tracing.
RUM resources tracking requires enabling DDURLSessionInstrumentation. See .
Note: Automatic RUM resources tracking involves swizzling the URLSession, URLSessionTask and URLSessionDataDelegate methods.

member.remarks: Default: NULL - which means automatic tracing is not enabled by default.

member.name: P:Datadog.iOS.DDTraceConfiguration.Tags
member.summary: Global tags associated with each span created with the default tracer.

member.name: T:Datadog.iOS.DDTraceContextInjection
member.summary: Defines whether the trace context should be injected into all requests or only sampled ones.

member.name: F:Datadog.iOS.DDTraceContextInjection.All
member.summary: Injects trace context into all requests irrespective of the sampling decision.

member.name: F:Datadog.iOS.DDTraceContextInjection.Sampled
member.summary: Injects trace context only into sampled requests.

member.name: T:Datadog.iOS.DDTraceFirstPartyHostsTracing
member.summary: Defines configuration for first-party hosts in distributed tracing.

member.name: C:Datadog.iOS.DDTraceFirstPartyHostsTracing(Foundation.NSDictionary{Foundation.NSString,Foundation.NSSet{Datadog.iOS.DDTracingHeaderType}})
member.param: Dictionary of hosts and tracing header types to use. Note: Hosts must not include http(s):// prefix.
member.summary: Trace given hosts with using custom tracing headers.

member.name: C:Datadog.iOS.DDTraceFirstPartyHostsTracing(Foundation.NSSet{Foundation.NSString})
member.param: The set of hosts to inject tracing headers. Note: Hosts must not include the http(s):// prefix.
member.summary: Trace the specified hosts using Datadog and W3C tracecontext tracing headers.

member.name: C:Datadog.iOS.DDTraceFirstPartyHostsTracing(Foundation.NSDictionary{Foundation.NSString,Foundation.NSSet{Datadog.iOS.DDTracingHeaderType}},System.Single)
member.param: Dictionary of hosts and tracing header types to use. Note: Hosts must not include http(s):// prefix.
The sampling rate for tracing. Must be a value between 0.0 and 100.0. Default: 20.
member.summary: Trace given hosts with using custom tracing headers.

member.name: C:Datadog.iOS.DDTraceFirstPartyHostsTracing(Foundation.NSSet{Foundation.NSString},System.Single)
member.param: The set of hosts to inject tracing headers. Note: Hosts must not include the http(s):// prefix.
The sampling rate for tracing. Must be a value between 0.0 and 100.0. Default: 20.
member.summary: Trace the specified hosts using Datadog and W3C tracecontext tracing headers.

member.name: T:Datadog.iOS.DDTracer
member.summary: A class for manual interaction with the Trace feature. It records spans that are sent to Datadog APM.

remarks.para: There can be only one active DDTracer for a certain instance of the Datadog SDK. It gets enabled along with the call to DDTrace.Enable(DDTraceConfiguration):

member.name: M:Datadog.iOS.DDTracer.Inject(Datadog.iOS.IOTSpanContext,System.String,Foundation.NSObject,Foundation.NSError@)
member.param: The instance to inject.
Must be the string returned by .
An instance of one of the following types: , , or .
In the event of an error, the instance.
member.summary: Transfer the span information into the carrier of the given format.

remarks.para: See Also: propagation (http://opentracing.io/propagation/)

member.name: P:Datadog.iOS.DDTracer.Shared
member.summary: Obtains the IOTTracer for manual tracing instrumentation.
member.value: The IOTTracer that conforms to the Open Tracing API ()
member.remarks: It requires DDTrace.Enable(DDTraceConfiguration) to be called first - otherwise it will return a no-op implementation.

member.name: M:Datadog.iOS.DDTracer.StartSpan(System.String)
member.param: the operation name for the newly-started span.
member.summary: Start a new span with the given operation name.
member.returns: A valid Span instance; it is the caller's responsibility to call .

member.name: M:Datadog.iOS.DDTracer.StartSpan(System.String,Datadog.iOS.IOTSpanContext)
member.param: the operation name for the newly-started span.
Span context that will be a parent reference. If no reference is provided, and an active span exists in the current execution context the active span will be used as the parent.
member.summary: Start a new span with the given operation name.
member.returns: A valid Span instance; it is the caller's responsibility to call .

member.name: M:Datadog.iOS.DDTracer.StartSpan(System.String,Foundation.NSDictionary)
member.param: the operation name for the newly-started span.
A set of tag keys and values per , or NULL to start with an empty tag map.
member.summary: Start a new span with the given operation name.
member.returns: A valid Span instance; it is the caller's responsibility to call .

member.name: M:Datadog.iOS.DDTracer.StartSpan(System.String,Datadog.iOS.IOTSpanContext,Foundation.NSDictionary)
member.param: the operation name for the newly-started span.
Span context that will be a parent reference. If no reference is provided, and an active span exists in the current execution context the active span will be used as the parent.
A set of tag keys and values per , or NULL to start with an empty tag map.
member.summary: Start a new span with the given operation name.
member.returns: A valid Span instance; it is the caller's responsibility to call .

member.name: M:Datadog.iOS.DDTracer.StartSpan(System.String,Datadog.iOS.IOTSpanContext,Foundation.NSDictionary,Foundation.NSDate)
member.param: the operation name for the newly-started span.
Span context that will be a parent reference. If no reference is provided, and an active span exists in the current execution context the active span will be used as the parent.
A set of tag keys and values per , or NULL to start with an empty tag map.
An explicitly specified start timestamp for the , or >NULL to use the current walltime.
member.summary: Start a new span with the given operation name.
member.returns: A valid Span instance; it is the caller's responsibility to call .

member.name: T:Datadog.iOS.DDTraceSamplingStrategy
member.summary: Available strategies for sampling trace propagation headers.

member.name: M:Datadog.iOS.DDTraceSamplingStrategy.Custom(System.Single)
member.summary: Trace propagation headers will be sampled independently from sampling decision in propagated span. Use this option to apply the provided SampleRate for determining the decision to keep or drop the trace in downstream services independently of sampling their parent span.

member.name: P:Datadog.iOS.DDTraceSamplingStrategy.HeadBased
member.summary: Trace propagation headers will be sampled same as propagated span. Use this option to leverage head-based sampling, where the decision to keep or drop the trace is determined from the first span of the trace, the head, when the trace is created. With the HeadBased strategy, this decision is propagated through the request context to downstream services.

member.name: T:Datadog.iOS.DDTraceURLSessionTracking
member.summary: Configuration of automatic network requests tracing.

member.name: M:Datadog.iOS.DDTraceURLSessionTracking.SetFirstPartyHostsTracing(Datadog.iOS.DDTraceFirstPartyHostsTracing)
member.summary: Determines distributed tracing configuration for particular first-party hosts.

remarks.para: Each request is classified as first-party or third-party based on the first-party hosts configured.
For example:

item.description: If example.com is defined as a first-party host:
First-party URL examples include https://example.com/ and https://api.example.com/v2/users.
Third-party URL example: https://foo.com/.
If api.example.com is defined as a first-party host:
First-party URL examples include https://api.example.com/ and >https://api.example.com/v2/users.
Third-party URL examples include https://example.com/ and https://foo.com/.

remarks.para: A trace will be created for each first-party request by injecting HTTP trace headers and creating an APM span. If your backend is also instrumented with Datadog, you will see the full trace (app → backend).

member.name: T:Datadog.iOS.DDTracingHeaderType
member.summary: The type of the tracing header injected to requests.

member.name: P:Datadog.iOS.DDTracingHeaderType.B3
member.summary: Open Telemetry B3 Single header (https://github.com/openzipkin/b3-propagation#single-headers)

member.name: P:Datadog.iOS.DDTracingHeaderType.B3Multi
member.summary: Open Telemetry B3 Multiple headers (https://github.com/openzipkin/b3-propagation#multiple-headers)

member.name: P:Datadog.iOS.DDTracingHeaderType.Datadog
member.summary: Datadog's x-datadog-*header. (https://docs.datadoghq.com/real_user_monitoring/connect_rum_and_traces/?tab=browserrum#how-are-rum-resources-linked-to-traces)

member.name: P:Datadog.iOS.DDTracingHeaderType.TraceContext
member.summary: W3C Trace Context header (https://www.w3.org/TR/trace-context/#tracestate-header)

member.name: T:Datadog.iOS.DDUploadFrequency
member.summary: Defines the frequency at which the Datadog SDK will try to upload data batches.

member.name: F:Datadog.iOS.DDUploadFrequency.Average
member.summary: Try to upload batched data with a medium frequency.

member.name: F:Datadog.iOS.DDUploadFrequency.Frequent
member.summary: Try to upload batched data frequently.

member.name: F:Datadog.iOS.DDUploadFrequency.Rare
member.summary: Try to upload batched data rarely.

member.name: M:Datadog.iOS.DDURLSessionInstrumentation.Disable``1
member.typeparam: An instance of INSUrlSessionDataDelegate. The delegate class to unbind.
member.summary: Disables URLSession instrumentation.

member.name: M:Datadog.iOS.DDURLSessionInstrumentation.Enable(Datadog.iOS.DDURLSessionInstrumentationConfiguration)
member.param: Configuration of the feature.
member.summary: Enables URLSession instrumentation.

member.name: T:Datadog.iOS.DDURLSessionInstrumentationConfiguration
member.summary: Configuration of URLSession instrumentation.

member.name: P:Datadog.iOS.DDURLSessionInstrumentationConfiguration.DelegateClass
member.summary: The delegate class to be used to swizzle URLSessionTaskDelegate & URLSessionDataDelegate methods.

member.name: M:Datadog.iOS.DDURLSessionInstrumentationConfiguration.SetFirstPartyHostsTracing(Datadog.iOS.DDURLSessionInstrumentationFirstPartyHostsTracing)
member.summary: Sets additional first party hosts to consider in the interception.

member.name: T:Datadog.iOS.DDURLSessionInstrumentationFirstPartyHostsTracing
member.summary: Defines configuration for first-party hosts in distributed tracing.

member.name: T:Datadog.iOS.DDW3CHTTPHeadersWriter
member.summary: The DDW3CHTTPHeadersWriter class facilitates the injection of trace propagation headers into network requests targeted at a backend expecting W3C propagation format.

remarks.para: See W3C Trace Context (https://www.w3.org/TR/trace-context/) for more details on the W3C propagation format.
Usage:

member.name: P:Datadog.iOS.DDW3CHTTPHeadersWriter.TraceHeaderFields
member.summary: A dictionary containing the required HTTP Headers for propagating trace information.

remarks.para: Usage:

member.name: M:Datadog.iOS.DDWebViewTracking.Enable(WebKit.WKWebView,Foundation.NSSet{Foundation.NSString},System.Single)
member.param: The web-view to track.
A set of hosts instrumented with Browser SDK to capture Datadog events from.
The sampling rate for logs coming from the WebView. Must be a value between 0 and 100, where 0 means no logs will be sent and 100 means all will be uploaded. Default: 100.
member.summary: Enables SDK to correlate Datadog RUM events and Logs from the WebView with native RUM session. If the content loaded in WebView uses Datadog Browser SDK (v4.2.0+) and matches specified hosts, web events will be correlated with the RUM session from native SDK.

member.name: T:Datadog.iOS.IDDDataEncryption
member.summary: Interface that allows storing data in encrypted format. Encryption/decryption round should return exactly the same data as it given for the encryption originally (even if decryption happens in another process/app launch).

member.name: M:Datadog.iOS.IDDDataEncryption.Decrypt(Foundation.NSData,Foundation.NSError@)
member.param: Data to decrypt.
member.summary: Decrypts given Data with user-chosen encryption.
member.remarks: Beware that data to decrypt could be encrypted in a previous app launch, so implementation should be aware of the case when decryption could fail (for example, key used for encryption is different from key used for decryption, if they are unique for every app launch).

member.name: M:Datadog.iOS.IDDDataEncryption.Encrypt(Foundation.NSData,Foundation.NSError@)
member.param: Data to encrypt.
member.summary: Encrypts given Data with user-chosen encryption.

member.name: T:Datadog.iOS.IDDServerDateProvider
member.summary: Abstract the monotonic clock synchronized with the server using NTP.

member.name: M:Datadog.iOS.IDDServerDateProvider.Synchronize(System.Action{System.Double})
member.summary: Start the clock synchronization with NTP server. Calls the Action by passing it the server time offset when the synchronization succeeds orNULL if it fails.

member.name: M:Datadog.iOS.IDDUIKitRUMViewsPredicate.RumView(UIKit.UIViewController)
member.param: An instance of the view controller noticed by the SDK.
member.summary: The predicate deciding if the RUM View should be started or ended for given instance of the UIViewController.
member.returns: RUM View parameters if received view controller should start/end the RUM View, NULL otherwise.

member.name: M:Datadog.iOS.IDDUIPressRUMActionsPredicate.RumAction(UIKit.UIPressType,UIKit.UIView)
member.param: The UIPress.PressType which received the action.
An instance of the UIView which received the action.
member.summary: The predicate deciding if the RUM Action should be recorded.
member.returns: RUM Action if it should be recorded, NULL otherwise.

member.name: M:Datadog.iOS.IDDUITouchRUMActionsPredicate.RumAction(UIKit.UIView)
member.param: An instance of the UIView which received the action.
member.summary: The predicate deciding if the RUM Action should be recorded.
member.returns: RUM Action if it should be recorded, NULL otherwise.

member.name: T:Datadog.iOS.IOTSpan
member.summary: Corresponds to: https://github.com/opentracing/opentracing-objc/blob/master/Pod/Classes/OTSpan.h
member.remarks: Represents information related to an event with a timespan.

member.name: P:Datadog.iOS.IOTSpan.Context
member.summary: The span context that refers to this span.

member.name: M:Datadog.iOS.IOTSpan.Finish(Foundation.NSDate)
member.param: If non-null, time at which to finish the span; default time is used if null.
member.summary: Finish the span at the specified time, or at some default time if null.

member.name: T:Datadog.iOS.IOTSpanContext
member.summary: Corresponds to: https://github.com/opentracing/opentracing-objc/blob/master/Pod/Classes/OTSpanContext.h

member.name: T:Datadog.iOS.IOTTracer
member.summary: Corresponds to: https://github.com/opentracing/opentracing-objc/blob/master/Pod/Classes/OTTracer.h

remarks.para: Tracer is the starting point for all OpenTracing instrumentation. Use it to create OTSpans, inject/extract them between processes, and so on.
Tracer should be thread-safe.

member.name: T:Datadog.iOS.IURLSessionDelegateProviding
member.summary: An interface for forwarding URLSessionDelegate calls to DDURLSessionDelegate. The implementation must ensure that required methods are called on the ddURLSessionDelegate.

member.name: P:Datadog.iOS.IURLSessionDelegateProviding.WeakDDURLSessionDelegate
member.summary: Datadog delegate object.

remarks.para: The class implementing DDURLSessionDelegateProviding must ensure that the following method calls are forwarded to DDURLSessionDelegate:
